violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving in the dairy/deli reach-in and the back storage cold hold unit have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/22/2024

Ambient temperature of the back storage cold hold unit observed at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Milk in this unit observed with temperature of 47 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Voluntary disposal at the time of this inspection. TCS foods are not to be stored in this unit until it is serviced and maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023

Accurate thermometer is lacking in the back storage refrigeration unit. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one accurate thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023
